ksa_text: Knowledge of the regulatory and compliance landscape governing enterprise data and AI systems, including data protection law, AI-specific regulation, sector-specific obligations, cross-jurisdictional requirements, and regulatory authority oversight mechanisms.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of laws, regulations, and policies related to AI, data security/privacy, and use of publicly procured data for government.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of intelligence laws and regulations

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Security & Platform Strategy is a primary or secondary domain for the VP AI/ML. This knowledge KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (vp).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Security & Platform Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.505). External reviewers may question whether this specific knowledge KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the VP AI/ML versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST CSF, CMU SEI enterprise security frameworks, and CDAIO job market data consistently include security and platform strategy oversight for senior data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Security & Platform Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for VP AI/ML.

decision_rationale: Security & Platform Strategy knowledge KSA is within scope for the VP AI/ML at the vp tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
